tex:
(a) 
t Calculate the volume of

(i)  a solid cylinder with radius 6\,cm and height 14\,cm,

 a solid hemisphere with radius 6\,cm.

[The volume, $V$, of a sphere with radius $r$ is $V = \frac{4}{3}\pi r^3$.]

t

The cylinder and hemisphere in **part (a)** are joined to form the solid in the diagram.

The solid is made of steel and 1\,cm$^3$ of steel has a mass of 7.85\,g.

(i)  Show that 1\,cm$^3$ of steel has a mass of 0.00785\,kg.

 Calculate the total mass of the solid.

t 2000\,cm$^3$ of iron is melted down and some of it is used to make 50 spheres with radius 2\,cm.

(i)  Calculate the percentage of iron that is left over.

[The volume, $V$, of a sphere with radius $r$ is $V = \frac{4}{3}\pi r^3$.]

 The iron left over is then made into a cube.

Calculate the length of an edge of the cube.

t A solid cone has radius $3R$\,cm and slant height $9R$\,cm.

A solid cylinder has radius $x$\,cm and height $7x$\,cm.

The **total** surface area of the cone is equal to the **total** surface area of the cylinder.

Given that $R = kx$, find the value of $k$.

[The curved surface area, $A$, of a cone with radius $r$ and slant height $l$ is $A = \pi rl$.]

(a) 
t Write

(i)  2994.99 correct to the nearest 10,

 0.983 correct to 1 decimal place,

 2090 correct to 2 significant figures.

t Write down a prime number between 90 and 100.

t Write $2^{-6}$ as a fraction.

t Write 0.00701 in standard form.

t Simplify $1.5 \times 10^x + 1.5 \times 10^{x-1}$ giving your answer in standard form.

t Write $0.3\dot{7}$ as a fraction.

You must show all your working.

(a) 
t

Write down the inequality shown by the number line.

t $-3 \leqslant 2x + 3 < 9$

(i)  Solve the inequality.

 Write down all the integer values of $x$ that satisfy the inequality.

t Solve the equations.

(i)  $\displaystyle 3(3 - x) - \frac{2(x + 2)}{5} = 1$

 $\displaystyle \frac{5}{x + 3} = \frac{3}{x + 5}$

(a) 
t
(i)  Zak invests \$500 at a rate of 2\% per year simple interest.

Calculate the value of Zak's investment at the end of 5 years.

 Yasmin invests \$500 at a rate of 1.8\% per year compound interest.

Calculate the value of Yasmin's investment at the end of 5 years.

 Zak and Yasmin continue with these investments.

How many **more complete** years is it before the value of Yasmin's investment is greater than the value of Zak's investment?

t Xavier buys a car for \$2500.

The value of the car decreases exponentially at a rate of 10\% each year.

Calculate the value of Xavier's car at the end of 5 years.

Give your answer correct to the nearest dollar.

t The number of a certain type of bacteria increases exponentially at a rate of $r$\% each day.

After 22 days, the number of this bacteria has doubled.

Find the value of $r$.

(a) 
t 100 students each record the time, $t$ minutes, taken to eat a pizza.

The cumulative frequency diagram shows the results.

Find an estimate of

(i)  the median,

 the interquartile range,

 the number of students taking more than 11 minutes to eat a pizza.

t 150 students each record how far they can throw a tennis ball.

The table shows the results.

\begin{tabular}{|c|c|c|c|c|c|}
\hline
Distance ($d$ metres) & $0 < d \leqslant 20$ & $20 < d \leqslant 30$ & $30 < d \leqslant 35$ & $35 < d \leqslant 45$ & $45 < d \leqslant 60$ \\
\hline
Frequency & 4 & 38 & 40 & 53 & 15 \\
\hline
\end{tabular}
(i)  Calculate an estimate of the mean.

 A histogram is drawn to show this information.

The height of the bar representing $30 < d \leqslant 35$ is 12\,cm.

Calculate the height of each of the other bars.

\begin{tabular}{|c|c|c|}
\hline
Distance ($d$ metres) & Frequency & Height of bar (cm) \\
\hline
$0 < d \leqslant 20$ & 4 & \\
\hline
$20 < d \leqslant 30$ & 38 & \\
\hline
$30 < d \leqslant 35$ & 40 & 12 \\
\hline
$35 < d \leqslant 45$ & 53 & \\
\hline
$45 < d \leqslant 60$ & 15 & \\
\hline
\end{tabular}

 Two students are chosen at random.

Find the probability that they both threw the ball more than 45\,m.

Two students are chosen at random. Find the probability that they both threw the ball more than 45\,m.

tex:
(a) 
t $\mathbf{p} = \begin{pmatrix} 2 \\ 3 \end{pmatrix}$ \qquad $\mathbf{q} = \begin{pmatrix} -1 \\ 1 \end{pmatrix}$

Find

(i)  $3\mathbf{q}$,

 $\mathbf{p} - \mathbf{q}$,

 $|\mathbf{p}|$.

t $B$ is the point $(2, 7)$ and $\overrightarrow{AB} = \begin{pmatrix} -4 \\ 6 \end{pmatrix}$.

Find the coordinates of $A$.

{---} ,  )][]

t

In triangle $OGH$, $M$ is the midpoint of $OH$ and $K$ divides $GH$ in the ratio $5:2$.

$\overrightarrow{OG} = \mathbf{g}$ and $\overrightarrow{OH} = \mathbf{h}$.

Find $\overrightarrow{MK}$ in terms of $\mathbf{g}$ and $\mathbf{h}$.

Give your answer in its simplest form.

\[\text{f}(x) = 10 - x \qquad \text{g}(x) = \frac{2}{x}, \; x \neq 0 \qquad \text{h}(x) = 2^x \qquad \text{j}(x) = 5 - 2x\]

(a) 
t
(i)  Find $\text{g}\left(\frac{1}{2}\right)$.

 Find $\text{hg}\left(\frac{1}{2}\right)$.

t Find $x$ when $\text{f}(x) = 7$.

t Find $x$ when $\text{g}(x) = \text{h}(3)$.

t Find $\text{j}^{-1}(x)$.

t Write $\text{f}(x) + \text{g}(x) + 1$ as a single fraction in its simplest form.

t $\left(\text{f}(x)\right)^2 - \text{ff}(x) = ax^2 + bx + c$

Find the values of $a$, $b$ and $c$.

t Find $x$ when $\text{h}^{-1}(x) = 10$.

The diagram shows triangle $ABC$ on horizontal ground.

$AC = 15$\,m, $BC = 8$\,m and $AB = 20$\,m.

$BP$ and $CQ$ are vertical poles of different heights.

$BP = 3$\,m and $CQ = 4$\,m.

$AQ$ and $PQ$ are straight wires.

(a) 
t Show that angle $ACB = 117.5°$, correct to 1 decimal place.

t Calculate the area of triangle $ABC$.

t Calculate the length of $AQ$.

t Calculate the angle of elevation of $Q$ from $P$.

t Another straight wire connects $A$ to the midpoint of $PQ$.

Calculate the angle between this wire and the horizontal ground.

(a) 
t

The total of the areas of rectangles $A$ and $B$ is 20\,cm$^2$.

(i)  Show that $3x^2 + 6x - 22 = 0$.

 Solve the equation $3x^2 + 6x - 22 = 0$, giving your answers correct to 4 significant figures.

You must show all your working.

{---} or $x =$][]

 Find the perimeter of rectangle $B$.

t

The diagram shows two rectangles where $H - h = 1$.

By forming a quadratic equation and factorising, find the value of $y$.

(a) 
t

The diagram shows a sketch of the graph of $y = \text{f}(x)$ for $-1.5 \leqslant x \leqslant 6$.

The coordinates of five points on the graph of $y = \text{f}(x)$ are shown on the diagram.

(i)  $\text{f}(x) = k$ has two solutions in the interval $-1.5 \leqslant x \leqslant 6$.

Write down a possible integer value of $k$.

 $\text{f}(x) = j$ has no solutions in the interval $-1.5 \leqslant x \leqslant 6$ when $j < a$ or $j > b$.

Find the maximum value of $a$ and the minimum value of $b$.

t Find the coordinates of the two stationary points on the graph of $y = x^6 - 6x^5$.

You must show all your working.
